sutta: once the exalted 1 was staying among the gurus at kama sadama the township of the kurus on that occasion the exalted 1 addressed the monks saying monks there are these 10 ways of aryan living or abiding according to which aryans have lived do live and shall live what can hearing among has abandoned 5 factors is possessed of 6 factors god's 1 factor observes the 4 bases or supports has got rid of individual beliefs or views has utterly given up longings his thoughts are unclouded his body conditioner is tranquilized he is well released by mind he is well released by wisdom these are the 10 ways of aryan living according to which aryans have lived do live and shall live i'll just stop here for a moment so here what the buddha is saying is that aryans practice these 10 things but these but it doesn't mean that all aryans possess these 10 things uh the highest aryan which is the arahant would possess all these 10 qualities but other aryans for example the sotapanna or sakura the garmin they would have slightly they may have slightly less than all these 10 qualities just like the noble eightfold path if a manga possess the noble eightfold path completely then he is an arahan but other aryans do not possess the noble eiffel path completely or perfectly and then the buddha continued [Music] and how is among 1 who has abandoned 5 factors hearing among has abandoned sensual desire ill will sloth and topper restlessness and worry doubt and wavering thus he is 1 who has abandoned 5 factors and how is among 1 who is possessed of 6 factors herein among seeing an object with the eye is not elated or depressed but dwells equanimous mindful and collected hearing a sound with the ear smelling ascent with a nose tasting a flavor with a tongue contacting an object with the body with mind cognizing mental states he is not elated or depressed but dwells equanimous mindful and collected thus is among possessed of 6 factors and how does a monk guard 1 factor by guarding recollection or sometimes they translate as mindfulness he is composed or collected thus he guards 1 factor and how does a monk observe the 4 bases herein among deliberately develops 1 thing deliberately nurtures or promotes another thing restrains 1 thing [Music] abandons or suppresses another thing thus among observes the 4 bases and how is among 1 who has got rid of individual beliefs or views iran monks whatsoever individual views generally prevail among recluses and brahmins namely the world is eternal the world is not eternal the world is finite it is infinite the soul and the body are the same the soul and the body are different exists after death but the targatha exists not after that or he both exists and yet exists not or he neither exists nor exists not after death all these views are shaken off put away given up let go abandoned and dismissed thus is among 1 who has got rid of individual views and how is among 1 who has utterly given up longings hearing in among longing for things sensual is abandoned longing for existence is abandoned longing for practicing the holy life has calmed down thus is among 1 who has utterly given up longings and how are amongst thoughts unclouded hearing among has abandoned thoughts sensual thoughts malicious thoughts of harming thus are his thoughts unclouded and how is a monk's body conditioner tranquilized hearing among by abandoning pleasure and pain by coming to an end of the ease and discomfort that which he had before attains and abides in the state of neither pain nor pleasure and equan and utter economy utter economy equanimity which is the fourth jhana thus his body conditioner is tranquilized and housed among well released by mind hearing amongst mind is released from passion hate and delusion or great agreed hatred and delusion and how is among well released by wisdom herein among knows for certain greed or passion is abandoned in me cut off at the root made like a palm tree stump made not to become again of a nature not to arise again in future hatred delusion is abandoned in me not to arise again in future time thus is among well released by wisdom monks whatsoever aryans have in the past lived according to the aryan living all of them lived according to these 10 ways of aryan living whatsoever aryans shall in future time so live whatsoever aarons do now so live all of them shall live and do live according to these 10 ways of aryan living these monks are the 10 ways of aryan living and that's the end of the suta
commentary: now it is explained the first 1 among has abandoned 5 factors that means sensual desire ill will sloth and topper restlessness and doubt now these 5 things are called the 5 hindrances and these are the things that obstruct wisdom the buddha said these are evil things only when you get rid of these 5 hindrances can you have wisdom because they obstruct you from seeing things clearly from seeing things as they really are now how do you get rid of these 5 hindrances in a suta in the majima nikaya the buddha did explain that you must attain the jhana to get rid of the 5 hindrances so the first 1 is to abandon the 5 hindrances the second 1 among is possessed of 6 factors seeing an object with the eye is not elated or depressed but dwells equanimous mindful and collected and similarly with hearing the sound smelling as smell etc so here it means guarding uh mindful of the sixth sense doors and practicing here in bali is called sato sampajano sato refers to sati having recollection and sam pajano means mindfulness or full awareness full awareness these 2 terms tend to come together because when you practice mindfulness and then you tend to have satie means having full awareness or mindfulness and sati is to remember to put your mind in the right direction at the 4 objects of satir then number 3 is to guard 1 factor here this factor is also sati sati recollection this is guarding 1 factor sati is quite important for a man who is practicing putting the mind on the body the feelings the mind and the dhamma and number 4 how uh is to observe the 4 bases these 4 bases are the 4 right efforts uh deliberately develop 1 thing i deliberately nurture or promote another thing restrain or avoid 1 thing abandon or suppress another thing this refers to wholesome states and unwholesome states the first 1 is uh wholesome states who some states that have not developed you must develop it and they have not arisen you have to develop it so that it arises and then wholesome states that have already arisen you should mature or promote it so that it continues to arise then unwholesome states that have not arisen you should avoid or restrain it from arising and unwholesome states which have already arisen you try to suppress it or abandon it so these are the full right efforts and number 5 the monk has got rid of individual views all kinds of views exist in the world that the world is eternal the world is not eternal the world is finite or infinite etc these all have to do with an ego once you have a view of a self then you tend to think about all these things like so the monk has got rid of all these views number 6 is to have given up longings here longings refers to sensual longings longing for existence and longing for practicing the holy life has calmed down the first 1 you can understand sensual of uh objects and longing for sensual things you're abandoned longing for existence also you abandon them when you become an arya but the third 1 longing for practicing the holy life has come down and this 1 looks a bit strange in the first suta of the sanguita nikaya the buddha said that some deva asked him how did you cross the flood and the buddha said not striving and not standing still i crossed the flood the buddha said when i strove i was swirled about when i stood still i sang thus without striving without standing still i crossed the flood what the buddha was meant was if he made too much effort then he was swirled around if you make too much effort but if you make no effort at all also that means he sang in the middle of the suppose a person is trying to cross the river you know he make too much effort he'll go in a circle so if he made no effort he'll be he'll sing in the water so the buddha said neither striving nor standing still he crossed the flood in other words to have an equanimous mind if you have too much desire to get liberated for a monk that is also bad having no desire to get liberated all so bad so uh the mind must be equanimous and then oniya he could uh cross the flood number 7 how are amongst thoughts unclouded a man among has abandoned 4 central thoughts malicious and thoughts of harming these are the wrong thoughts so he has abandoned wrong thoughts and so his thoughts are unclouded and number 9 his body conditioner is tranquilized this 1 um refers the body conditioner refers to the breath because the breath is conditioned by the sorry the body is conditioned by the breath and the body exists because of the because of the breath so the body conditioner remain refers to the breath and here it means the the breath has stopped because when the person attains the fourth jana the breath stops so here uh this aryan has attained the fourth jana and normally uh that is a condition for an anagaming and an arahant uh he must an anagami a third fruition and an arahant fourth fruition arya they must attain the 4 jaanas and then number 9 a monk is released by mind here he is released from greed hatred and delusion or passion hatred and delusion and then how is among released by wisdom also he is released from passion or greed hatred and delusion so these are the 10 things that aryans practice and and all they attain to become aryans some of them you find is uh refers only to the arahant for the for example the last 2 released or liberated by mind and released or liberated by wisdom this only refers to the arahant now the next suta is number 27
